# .github — full source bundle
# Generated 2026-04-06T06:00:42Z

########## FILE: actions/conventional-pr/action.yml ##########
# Usage:
#   - uses: actions/checkout@v4
#   - uses: ./.github/actions/validate-pr-title
#     with:
#       github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
#
# Usage (with extra scopes):
#   - uses: ./.github/actions/validate-pr-title
#     with:
#       github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
#       extra-scopes: "infra,release"
#
# Scopes auto-discovered from .moon/workspace.yml projects list.
# deps, ci, repo are always included.

name: "Validate PR Title"
description: "Enforce conventional commit format on PR titles with auto-discovered scopes"

inputs:
  github-token:
    required: true
    description: "GITHUB_TOKEN"
  extra-scopes:
    required: false
    default: ""
    description: "Comma-separated additional scopes beyond project names and builtins"

runs:
  using: composite
  steps:
    - name: Discover project scopes
      id: scopes
      shell: bash
      run: |
        projects=$(yq '.workspace.projects // .projects |
          if type == "!!seq" then .[] else keys[] end' .moon/workspace.yml 2>/dev/null | sort -u)

        builtins="deps,ci,repo"
        extra="${{ inputs.extra-scopes }}"
        combined="${builtins}${extra:+,$extra}"

        all_scopes=$(printf "%s\n%s" "$projects" "$(echo "$combined" | tr ',' '\n')" | sort -u)

        echo "scopes<<EOF" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        echo "$all_scopes" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        echo "EOF" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

    - uses: amannn/action-semantic-pull-request@v5
      env:
        GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ inputs.github-token }}
      with:
        types: |
          feat
          fix
          chore
          docs
          refactor
          test
          perf
        scopes: ${{ steps.scopes.outputs.scopes }}

########## END: actions/conventional-pr/action.yml ##########

########## FILE: actions/fuzz-cache/action.yml ##########
# .github/actions/fuzz-cache/action.yml
name: Fuzz Corpus Cache
description: >
  Restore/save per-project fuzz corpus with CI caching and a shared
  orphan branch. Self-initializing: creates the corpus branch on first push.
  PR mode is cache-only (no branch interaction).

inputs:
  mode:
    required: true
    description: "'pull' to restore cache + seed from branch, 'push' to sync corpus to branch"
  corpus-path:
    required: true
    description: "Repo-relative path to corpus directory (e.g. engine/fuzz/corpus)"
  pr-number:
    required: false
    default: ""
    description: "PR number (blank = main mode). Branch operations are skipped for PRs."
  corpus-branch:
    required: false
    default: "corpus"
    description: "Name of the orphan branch used for shared corpus storage"

outputs:
  cache-hit:
    description: "'true' if the CI cache was restored"
    value: ${{ steps.cache-main.outputs.cache-hit || steps.cache-pr.outputs.cache-hit || 'false' }}
  seeded:
    description: "Number of inputs seeded from corpus branch (0 for PR mode)"
    value: ${{ steps.seed.outputs.count || '0' }}
  pushed:
    description: "'true' if corpus was pushed to the branch"
    value: ${{ steps.push.outputs.pushed || 'false' }}

runs:
  using: composite
  steps:
    # ── Pull: restore CI cache ──────────────────────────────
    - id: cache-main
      if: inputs.mode == 'pull' && inputs.pr-number == ''
      uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: ${{ inputs.corpus-path }}
        key: fuzz-corpus-main-${{ inputs.corpus-path }}-${{ github.run_id }}
        restore-keys: |
          fuzz-corpus-main-${{ inputs.corpus-path }}-

    - id: cache-pr
      if: inputs.mode == 'pull' && inputs.pr-number != ''
      uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: ${{ inputs.corpus-path }}
        key: fuzz-corpus-pr-${{ inputs.pr-number }}-${{ inputs.corpus-path }}-${{ github.run_id }}
        restore-keys: |
          fuzz-corpus-pr-${{ inputs.pr-number }}-${{ inputs.corpus-path }}-
          fuzz-corpus-main-${{ inputs.corpus-path }}-

    # ── Pull: seed from orphan branch (main only) ──────────
    - id: seed
      if: inputs.mode == 'pull' && inputs.pr-number == ''
      shell: bash
      run: |
        CORPUS="${{ inputs.corpus-path }}"
        BRANCH="${{ inputs.corpus-branch }}"
        SEED="/tmp/corpus-seed"

        if ! git fetch origin "$BRANCH" --depth=1 2>/dev/null; then
          echo "No ${BRANCH} branch found, skipping seed"
          echo "count=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          exit 0
        fi

        git worktree add "$SEED" "origin/${BRANCH}" --detach 2>/dev/null || {
          echo "count=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          exit 0
        }

        SRC="${SEED}/${CORPUS}"
        if [[ -d "$SRC" ]]; then
          mkdir -p "$CORPUS"
          rsync -a "${SRC}/" "${CORPUS}/"
          COUNT=$(find "$SRC" -type f | wc -l)
          echo "Seeded ${COUNT} inputs from ${BRANCH} branch"
          echo "count=${COUNT}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        else
          echo "No corpus at ${CORPUS} on ${BRANCH} branch"
          echo "count=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        fi

        git worktree remove "$SEED" --force

    # ── Push: sync corpus to orphan branch (main only) ─────
    - id: push
      if: inputs.mode == 'push' && inputs.pr-number == ''
      shell: bash
      run: |
        CORPUS="${{ inputs.corpus-path }}"
        BRANCH="${{ inputs.corpus-branch }}"
        REMOTE="https://x-access-token:${GITHUB_TOKEN}@github.com/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY}.git"
        PUSH="/tmp/corpus-push"

        if ! git clone --depth=1 --branch "$BRANCH" --single-branch "$REMOTE" "$PUSH" 2>/dev/null; then
          echo "Branch ${BRANCH} not found, creating..."
          mkdir -p "$PUSH"
          cd "$PUSH"
          git init -q
          git remote add origin "$REMOTE"
          git checkout --orphan "$BRANCH"
          echo "Fuzz corpus storage. Do not merge into main." > README.md
          git add README.md
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git commit -q -m "init ${BRANCH} branch"
          git push -u origin "$BRANCH"
          cd -
        fi

        mkdir -p "${PUSH}/${CORPUS}"
        rsync -a --delete "${CORPUS}/" "${PUSH}/${CORPUS}/"

        cd "$PUSH"
        git add -A
        if git diff --cached --quiet; then
          echo "Corpus unchanged"
          echo "pushed=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          exit 0
        fi

        git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
        git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
        COUNT=$(git diff --cached --numstat | wc -l)
        git commit -q -m "corpus: ${CORPUS} ${COUNT} files $(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"

        for attempt in 1 2 3; do
          git push origin "$BRANCH" && break
          sleep $((attempt * 2))
          git pull --rebase origin "$BRANCH"
        done

        echo "Pushed ${COUNT} changed files"
        echo "pushed=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

        rm -rf "$PUSH"
      env:
        GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}

    #     # main-fuzz.yml saturate job
    # steps:
    #   # ... checkout, setup, resolve paths ...

    #   - uses: ./.github/actions/fuzz-cache
    #     with:
    #       mode: pull
    #       project-id: ${{ matrix.project.id }}
    #       corpus-path: ${{ steps.paths.outputs.corpus }}

    #   - name: Fuzz
    #     id: fuzz
    #     continue-on-error: true
    #     uses: ./.github/actions/moon-run
    #     with:
    #       task: ${{ matrix.project.id }}:fuzz-saturate

    #   - name: Triage
    #     if: steps.fuzz.outcome == 'failure'
    #     uses: ./.github/actions/moon-run
    #     with:
    #       task: ${{ matrix.project.id }}:fuzz-triage

    #   - uses: ./.github/actions/fuzz-cache
    #     if: always() && steps.fuzz.outcome != 'skipped'
    #     with:
    #       mode: push
    #       project-id: ${{ matrix.project.id }}
    #       corpus-path: ${{ steps.paths.outputs.corpus }}

    #   # ... upload regressions, fail if crashes ...






    #   # pr-fuzz.yml saturate job (push silently skips because pr-number is set)
    #   - uses: ./.github/actions/fuzz-cache
    #     with:
    #       mode: pull
    #       project-id: ${{ matrix.project.id }}
    #       corpus-path: ${{ steps.paths.outputs.corpus }}
    #       pr-number: ${{ needs.discover.outputs.pr-number }}

    #   # ... fuzz, triage ...

    #   - uses: ./.github/actions/fuzz-cache
    #     if: always() && steps.fuzz.outcome != 'skipped'
    #     with:
    #       mode: push
    #       project-id: ${{ matrix.project.id }}
    #       corpus-path: ${{ steps.paths.outputs.corpus }}
    #       pr-number: ${{ needs.discover.outputs.pr-number }}
########## END: actions/fuzz-cache/action.yml ##########

########## FILE: actions/git-commit-push/action.yml ##########
# .github/actions/git-commit-push/action.yml
name: Git Commit Push
description: >
  Stage, commit, and push changes to the current branch.
  Defaults the commit author to the last non-merge committer on the branch.

inputs:
  add-paths:
    required: true
    description: "Git add pathspec (e.g. '**/regressions/')"
  message:
    required: true
    description: "Commit message"

outputs:
  committed:
    description: "'true' if changes were committed and pushed"
    value: ${{ steps.commit.outputs.committed }}
  file-count:
    description: "Number of files committed"
    value: ${{ steps.commit.outputs.file-count }}

runs:
  using: composite
  steps:
    - id: commit
      shell: bash
      run: |
        git add --all "${{ inputs.add-paths }}"
        if git diff --cached --quiet; then
          echo "committed=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "file-count=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          exit 0
        fi

        AUTHOR_NAME=$(git log --no-merges -1 --format='%an' 2>/dev/null)
        AUTHOR_EMAIL=$(git log --no-merges -1 --format='%ae' 2>/dev/null)

        git config user.name "${AUTHOR_NAME:-github-actions[bot]}"
        git config user.email "${AUTHOR_EMAIL:-github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com}"

        FILECOUNT=$(git diff --cached --numstat | wc -l)
        git commit -m "${{ inputs.message }}"
        git push

        echo "committed=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        echo "file-count=${FILECOUNT}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

########## END: actions/git-commit-push/action.yml ##########

########## FILE: actions/git-stage-artifacts/action.yml ##########
# .github/actions/git-stage-artifacts/action.yml
name: Stage Artifacts
description: >
  Download patterned workflow artifacts and copy files into
  target directories based on a provided mapping.

inputs:
  artifact-prefix:
    required: true
    description: "Artifact name prefix to match (e.g. fuzz-regressions). Each artifact is expected to be named <prefix>-<id>."
  targets:
    required: true
    description: "JSON object mapping artifact IDs to target directory paths (e.g. {\"engine-fuzz\": \"engine/fuzz/regressions\"})"

outputs:
  file-count:
    description: "Total number of files staged"
    value: ${{ steps.stage.outputs.file-count }}
  projects:
    description: "Comma-separated list of IDs that had files staged"
    value: ${{ steps.stage.outputs.projects }}

runs:
  using: composite
  steps:
    - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
      with:
        pattern: ${{ inputs.artifact-prefix }}-*
        path: /tmp/staged-artifacts

    - id: stage
      shell: bash
      run: |
        shopt -s nullglob
        TARGETS='${{ inputs.targets }}'
        PREFIX="${{ inputs.artifact-prefix }}"
        FILECOUNT=0
        PROJECTS=""

        for dir in /tmp/staged-artifacts/${PREFIX}-*/; do
          ID=$(basename "$dir" | sed "s/^${PREFIX}-//")
          TARGET=$(echo "$TARGETS" | jq -r --arg id "$ID" '.[$id] // empty')

          if [[ -z "$TARGET" ]]; then continue; fi

          COUNT=$(find "$dir" -type f | wc -l)
          if [[ "$COUNT" -eq 0 ]]; then continue; fi

          mkdir -p "$TARGET"
          cp -n "$dir"/* "$TARGET/"
          FILECOUNT=$((FILECOUNT + COUNT))
          PROJECTS="${PROJECTS:+${PROJECTS}, }${ID}"
        done

        echo "file-count=${FILECOUNT}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        echo "projects=${PROJECTS}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

        rm -rf /tmp/staged-artifacts
########## END: actions/git-stage-artifacts/action.yml ##########

########## FILE: actions/moon-ci/action.yml ##########
# Usage:
#   - uses: ./.github/actions/moon-ci
#     with:
#       access-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

name: "Moon CI"
description: "Run moon ci with sharding, reporting, and retrospect"

inputs:
  access-token:
    required: true
    description: "GITHUB_TOKEN for run report"

runs:
  using: composite
  steps:
    - name: Moon CI
      shell: bash
      run: |
        echo "Runner ${{ strategy.job-index }} of ${{ strategy.job-total }}"
        moon ci --color --job ${{ strategy.job-index }} --job-total ${{ strategy.job-total }}

    - uses: moonrepo/run-report-action@v1
      if: success() || failure()
      with:
        access-token: ${{ inputs.access-token }}
        matrix: '{"index": ${{ strategy.job-index }}, "total": ${{ strategy.job-total }}}'

    - uses: appthrust/moon-ci-retrospect@v1
      if: success() || failure()

########## END: actions/moon-ci/action.yml ##########

########## FILE: actions/moon-ci-matrix/action.yml ##########
name: "Moon CI Runner Matrix"
description: "Calculates optimal runner count from affected Moon tasks"

inputs:
  tasks-per-runner:
    required: false
    default: "10"
    description: "Number of affected tasks to assign per runner"
  max-parallel:
    required: false
    default: "4"
    description: "Maximum number of concurrent runners"

outputs:
  matrix:
    description: "JSON array of shard indices, e.g. [0, 1, 2]"
    value: ${{ steps.calc.outputs.matrix }}
  count:
    description: "Total number of runners"
    value: ${{ steps.calc.outputs.count }}

runs:
  using: composite
  steps:
    - id: calc
      shell: bash
      run: |
        TASKS_PER_RUNNER=${{ inputs.tasks-per-runner }}
        MAX_RUNNERS=${{ inputs.max-parallel }}

        TASK_COUNT=$(moon query tasks --affected | jq '[.tasks[][] | select(.options.runInCI != false)] | length')

        WANTED=$(( (TASK_COUNT + TASKS_PER_RUNNER - 1) / TASKS_PER_RUNNER ))
        COUNT=$(( WANTED > MAX_RUNNERS ? MAX_RUNNERS : WANTED ))

        MATRIX_JSON=$(jq -n -c --argjson n "$COUNT" '[range($n)]')

        echo "Affected CI tasks: $TASK_COUNT, Runners: $COUNT"
        echo "count=$COUNT" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        echo "matrix=$MATRIX_JSON" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

########## END: actions/moon-ci-matrix/action.yml ##########

########## FILE: actions/moon-ci-matrix/notes.md ##########
Smarter matrix would inlcude task to runner affinity by toolchain

# Get affected projects with their language
moon query projects --affected --json | jq '[.projects[] | {id, language}] | group_by(.language)'

Then your matrix becomes something like:

json
[
  {"toolchain": "rust", "projects": ["parser", "wasm-bindings"]},
  {"toolchain": "javascript", "projects": ["web", "docs"]}
]

Each shard runs moon ci scoped to its projects, so cargo only compiles once on the Rust runner and node_modules only installs on the JS runner.

Moon's query commands give you a lot to work with:

    moon query projects --affected --json for affected projects with metadata

    moon query tasks --affected --json for affected tasks

    moon project-graph --json for the full dependency graph

You could even get fancier and look at the dependency graph to keep projects that depend on each other on the same runner. But grouping by language/toolchain gets you 90% of the benefit with minimal complexity. Worth prototyping once you have more than one project type in the repo.
########## END: actions/moon-ci-matrix/notes.md ##########

########## FILE: actions/moon-q-projects/action.yml ##########
# .github/actions/moon-q-projects/action.yml
name: Moon Q Projects
description: >
  Discover Moon projects by task name, tag, or both.
  Returns project IDs, count, and full results with metadata
  for use in matrix strategies and downstream steps.

inputs:
  task:
    required: false
    default: ""
    description: "Filter to projects that define this task (e.g. fuzz-ci, publish, benchmark)"
  tag:
    required: false
    default: ""
    description: "Filter to projects with this tag (e.g. fuzz, rust, deployable)"
  affected:
    required: false
    default: ""
    description: "'true' to diff against default branch, 'remote' for remote tracking branch, '' for no filtering"

outputs:
  project-ids:
    description: "Newline-separated list of matching project IDs (e.g. engine-fuzz, sdk-fuzz)"
    value: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.project-ids }}
  count:
    description: "Number of matching projects (e.g. 2)"
    value: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.count }}
  results:
    description: "JSON array of {id, source, metadata} objects for use in strategy.matrix"
    value: ${{ steps.discover.outputs.results }}

runs:
  using: composite
  steps:
    - id: discover
      shell: bash
      run: |
        TASK="${{ inputs.task }}"
        TAG="${{ inputs.tag }}"
        AFFECTED="${{ inputs.affected }}"

        FLAGS=""
        if [[ "$AFFECTED" == "remote" ]]; then
          FLAGS="$FLAGS --affected remote"
        elif [[ "$AFFECTED" == "true" ]]; then
          FLAGS="$FLAGS --affected"
        fi
        [[ -n "$TASK" ]] && FLAGS="$FLAGS --tasks $TASK"
        [[ -n "$TAG" ]] && FLAGS="$FLAGS --tags $TAG"

        RESULTS=$(moon query projects --json $FLAGS \
          | jq -c '[.projects[] | {
              id: .id,
              source: .source,
              metadata: (.config?.project?.metadata // {})
            }]')

        COUNT=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq 'length')
        PROJECT_IDS=$(echo "$RESULTS" | jq -r '.[].id')

        echo "results=${RESULTS}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        echo "count=${COUNT}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        echo "project-ids<<EOF" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        echo "$PROJECT_IDS" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        echo "EOF" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

        echo "Discovered ${COUNT} projects: $(echo "$PROJECT_IDS" | paste -sd', ' -)"
########## END: actions/moon-q-projects/action.yml ##########

########## FILE: actions/moon-raa/action.yml ##########
name: "moon-raa"
description: "Manage review apps and artifacts for a Moon monorepo"

inputs:
  command:
    required: true
    description: "'run' or 'rm'"
  pr-number:
    required: true
    description: "PR number"
  prefix:
    required: false
    default: "raa"
    description: "Environment name prefix (prepended to '-pr-{number}')"
  github-token:
    required: true
    description: "GITHUB_TOKEN"

outputs:
  deployment-id:
    description: "GitHub Deployment ID (run only)"
    value: ${{ steps.raa.outputs.deployment-id }}
  environment:
    description: "Environment name"
    value: ${{ steps.raa.outputs.environment }}
  project-count:
    description: "Number of projects processed"
    value: ${{ steps.raa.outputs.project-count }}
  summary:
    description: "Markdown summary for GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"
    value: ${{ steps.raa.outputs.summary }}
  manifest:
    description: "Full payload as JSON"
    value: ${{ steps.raa.outputs.manifest }}
  clean:
    description: "true if rm completed without warnings (rm only)"
    value: ${{ steps.raa.outputs.clean }}

runs:
  using: composite
  steps:
    - name: moon-raa
      id: raa
      uses: actions/github-script@v7
      env:
        INPUT_COMMAND: ${{ inputs.command }}
        INPUT_PR_NUMBER: ${{ inputs.pr-number }}
        INPUT_PREFIX: ${{ inputs.prefix }}
      with:
        github-token: ${{ inputs.github-token }}
        script: |
          const cmd = process.env.INPUT_COMMAND;
          const handler = require(`${{ github.action_path }}/${cmd}.js`);
          await handler({ github, context, core, exec, inputs: {
            pr_number: process.env.INPUT_PR_NUMBER,
            prefix: process.env.INPUT_PREFIX,
          }});

########## END: actions/moon-raa/action.yml ##########

########## FILE: actions/moon-run/action.yml ##########
# .github/actions/moon-run/action.yml
#
# Usage (affected against remote):
#   - uses: ./.github/actions/moon-run
#     with:
#       task: ":lint"
#       affected: "remote"
#
# Usage (no affected, exact task):
#   - uses: ./.github/actions/moon-run
#     with:
#       task: "slash-rust:publish"
#
# Usage (multiple tasks):
#   - uses: ./.github/actions/moon-run
#     with:
#       task: ":lint :test :fmt-check"
#       affected: "remote"

name: "Moon Run"
description: "Run a moon task with standard defaults and retrospect"

inputs:
  task:
    required: true
    description: "Moon task(s) to run (e.g., ':lint' or 'riff-cli:publish')"
  affected:
    required: false
    default: ""
    description: "'remote' = compare against remote tracking branch, 'true' = compare against default branch, '' = no affected filtering"

runs:
  using: composite
  steps:
    - name: Moon Run
      shell: bash
      run: |
        affected_flag=""
        if [ "${{ inputs.affected }}" = "remote" ]; then
          affected_flag="--affected remote"
        elif [ "${{ inputs.affected }}" = "true" ]; then
          affected_flag="--affected"
        fi
        moon run --color ${{ inputs.task }} $affected_flag

    - uses: appthrust/moon-ci-retrospect@v1
      if: success() || failure()

    - run: echo "${{ steps.raa.outputs.summary }}" >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY
      shell: bash
      if: always()

########## END: actions/moon-run/action.yml ##########

########## FILE: actions/pr-create/action.yml ##########
# .github/actions/pr-create/action.yml
name: PR Create
description: Commit changes and open or update a PR idempotently

inputs:
  add-paths:
    required: true
    description: "Git add pathspec"
  branch:
    required: true
    description: "Branch name (stable, no timestamp)"
  title:
    required: true
    description: "PR title (also used as commit message)"
  body:
    required: true
    description: "PR body markdown"
  github-token:
    required: true
    description: "GITHUB_TOKEN"

outputs:
  has-changes:
    description: "'true' if changes were committed"
    value: ${{ steps.commit.outputs.has-changes }}
  file-count:
    description: "Number of files committed"
    value: ${{ steps.commit.outputs.file-count }}
  pr-number:
    description: "PR number (new or existing)"
    value: ${{ steps.pr.outputs.number }}

runs:
  using: composite
  steps:
    - id: commit
      shell: bash
      run: |
        git add --all '${{ inputs.add-paths }}'
        if git diff --cached --quiet; then
          echo "has-changes=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "file-count=0" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          exit 0
        fi
        git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
        git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
        FILECOUNT=$(git diff --cached --numstat | wc -l)
        echo "has-changes=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        echo "file-count=${FILECOUNT}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

    - id: pr
      if: steps.commit.outputs.has-changes == 'true'
      shell: bash
      run: |
        BRANCH="${{ inputs.branch }}"
        EXISTING=$(gh pr list --head "$BRANCH" --json number -q '.[0].number' 2>/dev/null || echo "")

        if [[ -n "$EXISTING" ]]; then
          echo "Updating existing PR #${EXISTING}"
          git checkout "$BRANCH" 2>/dev/null || git checkout -b "$BRANCH"
          git commit -m "${{ inputs.title }}"
          git push --force-with-lease origin "$BRANCH"
          gh pr edit "$EXISTING" --title "${{ inputs.title }}" --body "${{ inputs.body }}"
          echo "number=${EXISTING}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        else
          git checkout -b "$BRANCH"
          git commit -m "${{ inputs.title }}"
          git push origin "$BRANCH"
          PR_NUMBER=$(gh pr create \
            --title "${{ inputs.title }}" \
            --body "${{ inputs.body }}" \
            --head "$BRANCH" \
            | grep -oP '#\K[0-9]+' || gh pr list --head "$BRANCH" --json number -q '.[0].number')
          echo "number=${PR_NUMBER}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        fi
      env:
        GH_TOKEN: ${{ inputs.github-token }}

########## END: actions/pr-create/action.yml ##########

########## FILE: actions/pr-set-status/action.yml ##########
# .github/actions/pr-status/action.yml
name: PR Set Status
description: Set commit status and post/update a sticky PR comment

inputs:
  sha:
    required: true
    description: "Git SHA"
  pr-number:
    required: true
    description: "PR Number"
  state:
    required: true
    description: "pending, success, or failure"
  context:
    required: true
    description: "Status check name (e.g. 'fuzz / saturate')"
  description:
    required: true
    description: "Description"
  comment-marker:
    required: true
    description: "HTML comment marker for sticky comment (e.g. '<!-- fuzz -->')"
  comment-body:
    required: false
    default: ""
    description: "Markdown body for PR comment (empty = no comment)"
  github-token:
    required: true
    description: "GITHUB_TOKEN"

runs:
  using: composite
  steps:
    - uses: actions/github-script@v7
      with:
        github-token: ${{ inputs.github-token }}
        script: |
          const runUrl = `${process.env.GITHUB_SERVER_URL}/${context.repo.owner}/${context.repo.repo}/actions/runs/${context.runId}`;

          await github.rest.repos.createCommitStatus({
            ...context.repo,
            sha: '${{ inputs.sha }}',
            state: '${{ inputs.state }}',
            context: '${{ inputs.context }}',
            description: '${{ inputs.description }}',
            target_url: runUrl,
          });

          const body = `${{ inputs.comment-body }}`;
          if (!body) return;

          const marker = '${{ inputs.comment-marker }}';
          const pr = Number('${{ inputs.pr-number }}');
          const { data: comments } = await github.rest.issues.listComments({
            ...context.repo,
            issue_number: pr,
            per_page: 100,
          });
          const existing = comments.find(c => c.body.startsWith(marker));

          const fullBody = marker + '\n' + body;
          if (existing) {
            await github.rest.issues.updateComment({
              ...context.repo, comment_id: existing.id, body: fullBody,
            });
          } else {
            await github.rest.issues.createComment({
              ...context.repo, issue_number: pr, body: fullBody,
            });
          }

########## END: actions/pr-set-status/action.yml ##########

########## FILE: actions/resolve-pr/action.yml ##########
# .github/actions/resolve-pr/action.yml
name: Resolve PR
description: >
  Normalize PR metadata from either a pull_request event or a
  workflow_dispatch with a pr-number input. Outputs number, ref, and sha.

inputs:
  pr-number:
    required: false
    default: ""
    description: "PR number (workflow_dispatch). Leave empty for pull_request events."
  github-token:
    required: true
    description: "GITHUB_TOKEN for gh CLI lookups"

outputs:
  number:
    description: "PR number"
    value: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.number }}
  ref:
    description: "Head branch name"
    value: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.ref }}
  sha:
    description: "Head SHA"
    value: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.sha }}

runs:
  using: composite
  steps:
    - id: resolve
      shell: bash
      run: |
        if [[ -n "$PR_EVENT_NUMBER" ]]; then
          echo "number=${PR_EVENT_NUMBER}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "ref=${PR_EVENT_REF}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "sha=${PR_EVENT_SHA}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        elif [[ -n "${{ inputs.pr-number }}" ]]; then
          NUMBER="${{ inputs.pr-number }}"
          PR_JSON=$(gh pr view "$NUMBER" --repo "$GITHUB_REPOSITORY" --json headRefName,headRefOid)
          echo "number=${NUMBER}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "ref=$(echo "$PR_JSON" | jq -r .headRefName)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "sha=$(echo "$PR_JSON" | jq -r .headRefOid)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        else
          echo "::error::No PR context. Provide pr-number input or trigger from a pull_request event."
          exit 1
        fi
      env:
        GH_TOKEN: ${{ inputs.github-token }}
        PR_EVENT_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
        PR_EVENT_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
        PR_EVENT_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
########## END: actions/resolve-pr/action.yml ##########

########## FILE: actions/setup/action.yml ##########
# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://json.schemastore.org/github-action.json
#
# .github/actions/setup/action.yml
#
# Usage:
#   - uses: ./.github/actions/setup
#     with:
#       cache-cargo: "true"
#       cache-pnpm: "false"
#
# Usage (minimal, no project caches):
#   - uses: ./.github/actions/setup

name: "Setup"
description: "Checkout, setup Moon/proto (lazy install), restore caches"

inputs:
  fetch-depth:
    default: "0"
    description: "Git fetch depth (0 for full history, needed for moon ci affected detection)"
  cache-cargo:
    default: "true"
    description: "Restore Cargo registry, git, and target caches"
  cache-pnpm:
    default: "true"
    description: "Restore pnpm store cache"

runs:
  using: composite
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      with:
        fetch-depth: ${{ inputs.fetch-depth }}

    - uses: actions/cache@v4
      if: inputs.cache-cargo == 'true'
      with:
        path: |
          ~/.cargo/bin
          ~/.cargo/registry
          ~/.cargo/git
          target
        key: cargo-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock') }}
        restore-keys: |
          cargo-${{ runner.os }}-

    - name: Install cargo-binstall
      shell: bash
      run: command -v cargo-binstall || curl -L --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://raw.githubusercontent.com/cargo-bins/cargo-binstall/main/install-from-binstall-release.sh | bash

    - uses: actions/cache@v4
      with:
        path: .moon/cache
        key: moon-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('.moon/workspace.yml', '.moon/tasks/*.yml', '.moon/toolchains.yml') }}
        restore-keys: |
          moon-${{ runner.os }}-

    - uses: actions/cache@v4
      if: inputs.cache-pnpm == 'true'
      with:
        path: ~/.local/share/pnpm/store
        key: pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-${{ hashFiles('**/pnpm-lock.yaml') }}
        restore-keys: |
          pnpm-${{ runner.os }}-

    - uses: moonrepo/setup-toolchain@v0
      with:
        auto-install: true

########## END: actions/setup/action.yml ##########

########## FILE: workflows/main-fuzz.yml ##########
# .github/workflows/main-fuzz.yml
name: Main Fuzz Saturate

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
  schedule:
    - cron: "0 2 * * 6"

env:
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true

concurrency:
  group: fuzz-main
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  discover:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    outputs:
      results: ${{ steps.q.outputs.results }}
      count: ${{ steps.q.outputs.count }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1
          cache-pnpm: false
      - id: q
        uses: ./.github/actions/moon-q-projects
        with:
          task: fuzz-ci

  saturate:
    needs: discover
    if: fromJson(needs.discover.outputs.count) > 0
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 360
    permissions:
      contents: write
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        project: ${{ fromJson(needs.discover.outputs.results) }}
    env:
      FUZZ_MAXTIME: "14400"
      FUZZ_JOBS: "4"

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1
          cache-pnpm: false

      - name: Resolve paths
        id: paths
        run: |
          SOURCE="${{ matrix.project.source }}"
          CORPUS="${{ matrix.project.metadata.corpus-path }}"
          REGRESSIONS="${{ matrix.project.metadata.regressions-path }}"
          echo "corpus=${SOURCE}/${CORPUS:-corpus}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "regressions=${SOURCE}/${REGRESSIONS:-regressions}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - uses: ./.github/actions/fuzz-cache
        with:
          mode: pull
          project-id: ${{ matrix.project.id }}
          corpus-path: ${{ steps.paths.outputs.corpus }}

      - name: Fuzz
        id: fuzz
        continue-on-error: true
        uses: ./.github/actions/moon-run
        with:
          task: ${{ matrix.project.id }}:fuzz-ci

      - uses: ./.github/actions/fuzz-cache
        if: always() && steps.fuzz.outcome != 'skipped'
        with:
          mode: push
          project-id: ${{ matrix.project.id }}
          corpus-path: ${{ steps.paths.outputs.corpus }}

      - name: Triage
        if: steps.fuzz.outcome == 'failure'
        uses: ./.github/actions/moon-run
        with:
          task: ${{ matrix.project.id }}:fuzz-triage

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: steps.fuzz.outcome == 'failure'
        with:
          name: fuzz-regressions-${{ matrix.project.id }}
          path: ${{ steps.paths.outputs.regressions }}/
          retention-days: 90

      - name: Fail if crashes
        if: steps.fuzz.outcome == 'failure'
        run: exit 1

  create-pr:
    needs: [discover, saturate]
    if: failure()
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Build target map
        id: targets
        run: |
          TARGETS=$(echo '${{ needs.discover.outputs.results }}' \
            | jq -c '[.[] | {
                key: .id,
                value: (.source + "/" + (.metadata["regressions-path"] // "regressions"))
              }] | from_entries')
          echo "map=${TARGETS}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: stage
        uses: ./.github/actions/git-stage-artifacts
        with:
          artifact-prefix: fuzz-regressions
          targets: ${{ steps.targets.outputs.map }}

      - name: Build PR metadata
        if: steps.stage.outputs.file-count != '0'
        id: meta
        run: |
          PROJECTS="${{ steps.stage.outputs.projects }}"
          FILECOUNT="${{ steps.stage.outputs.file-count }}"
          PROJECT_COUNT=$(echo "$PROJECTS" | tr ',' '\n' | wc -l)
          RUN_URL="${GITHUB_SERVER_URL}/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY}/actions/runs/${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"

          if [[ "$PROJECT_COUNT" -eq 1 ]]; then
            TITLE="chore(${PROJECTS}): add ${FILECOUNT} fuzz regression files"
          else
            TITLE="chore(fuzz): add ${FILECOUNT} regression files across ${PROJECTS}"
          fi

          {
            echo "title=${TITLE}"
            echo "body<<EOF"
            echo "Automated saturation run found new crashes."
            echo ""
            echo "**${FILECOUNT} regression files** across \`${PROJECTS}\`."
            echo "Triaged and minimized. Merge to add them to the test suite."
            echo ""
            echo "[View fuzz run](${RUN_URL})"
            echo "EOF"
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - uses: ./.github/actions/pr-create
        if: steps.stage.outputs.file-count != '0'
        with:
          add-paths: "**/regressions/"
          branch: chore/fuzz-regressions
          title: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.title }}
          body: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.body }}
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
########## END: workflows/main-fuzz.yml ##########

########## FILE: workflows/main.yml ##########
name: Trunk Pipeline

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

env:
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true

concurrency:
  group: trunk-${{ github.sha }}
  cancel-in-progress: false

jobs:
  config:
    name: CI Strategy
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      ci-matrix: ${{ steps.ci-matrix.outputs.matrix }}
      runner-count: ${{ steps.ci-matrix.outputs.count }}
    steps:
      # Shallow checkout so GH Actions can resolve local composite actions.
      # The setup action re-checks out with full history for moon's affected detection.
      # TODO: Remove once composite actions are published to a shared repo.
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup
      - id: ci-matrix
        uses: ./.github/actions/moon-ci-matrix

  ci:
    name: Moon CI
    needs: [config]
    if: ${{ fromJson(needs.config.outputs.runner-count || '0') > 0 }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: true
      matrix:
        shard: ${{ fromJson(needs.config.outputs.ci-matrix) }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup
      - uses: ./.github/actions/moon-ci
        with:
          access-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

  audit:
    name: Audit & SCA
    needs: [ci]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup
      - name: Run Moon Audit Tasks (OSV, Cargo, Gitleaks)
        run: moon run :audit

  codeql:
    name: CodeQL
    needs: [ci]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      security-events: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup
        with:
          cache-cargo: "false"
          cache-pnpm: "false"
      - uses: github/codeql-action/init@v3
        with:
          languages: javascript
      - uses: github/codeql-action/autobuild@v3
      - uses: github/codeql-action/analyze@v3

  release-please:
    name: Release Please
    needs: [ci, audit, codeql]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
    steps:
      - uses: googleapis/release-please-action@v4
        with:
          manifest-file: .release-please-manifest.json
          config-file: release-please-config.json

########## END: workflows/main.yml ##########

########## FILE: workflows/pr-close.yml ##########
name: PR Review Teardown

on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [closed]

env:
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true

jobs:
  teardown:
    name: Review Teardown
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      deployments: write
    steps:
      # Shallow checkout so GH Actions can resolve local composite actions.
      # The setup action re-checks out with full history for moon's affected detection.
      # TODO: Remove once composite actions are published to a shared repo.
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup

      - id: raa
        uses: ./.github/actions/moon-raa
        with:
          command: rm
          pr-number: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Summary
        if: always()
        run: echo '${{ steps.raa.outputs.summary }}' >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"

########## END: workflows/pr-close.yml ##########

########## FILE: workflows/pr-fuzz.yml ##########
# .github/workflows/pr-fuzz.yml
name: PR Fuzz Saturate

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      pr-number:
        description: PR number to fuzz
        type: string
        required: true
  pull_request:
    types: [labeled]

env:
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true

concurrency:
  group: fuzz-pr-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || inputs.pr-number }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  discover:
    if: github.event_name != 'pull_request' || github.event.label.name == 'fuzz'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 5
    permissions:
      statuses: write
      pull-requests: write
    outputs:
      pr-number: ${{ steps.resolve-pr.outputs.number }}
      ref: ${{ steps.resolve-pr.outputs.ref }}
      sha: ${{ steps.resolve-pr.outputs.sha }}
      results: ${{ steps.q.outputs.results }}
      count: ${{ steps.q.outputs.count }}

    steps:
      - id: resolve-pr
        uses: ./.github/actions/resolve-pr
        with:
          pr-number: ${{ inputs.pr-number }}
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ steps.resolve-pr.outputs.ref }}
          fetch-depth: 1

      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1
          cache-pnpm: false

      - uses: ./.github/actions/pr-set-status
        with:
          sha: ${{ steps.resolve-pr.outputs.sha }}
          pr-number: ${{ steps.resolve-pr.outputs.number }}
          state: pending
          context: "fuzz / saturate"
          description: "Fuzz saturation in progress..."
          comment-marker: "<!-- fuzz-saturate -->"
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - id: q
        uses: ./.github/actions/moon-q-projects
        with:
          task: fuzz-ci

  saturate:
    needs: discover
    if: fromJson(needs.discover.outputs.count) > 0
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 360
    permissions:
      contents: write
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        project: ${{ fromJson(needs.discover.outputs.results) }}
    env:
      FUZZ_MAXTIME: "18000"
      FUZZ_JOBS: "4"

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.discover.outputs.ref }}
          fetch-depth: 1

      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1
          cache-pnpm: false

      - name: Resolve paths
        id: paths
        run: |
          SOURCE="${{ matrix.project.source }}"
          CORPUS="${{ matrix.project.metadata.corpus-path }}"
          REGRESSIONS="${{ matrix.project.metadata.regressions-path }}"
          echo "corpus=${SOURCE}/${CORPUS:-corpus}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "regressions=${SOURCE}/${REGRESSIONS:-regressions}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - uses: ./.github/actions/fuzz-cache
        with:
          mode: pull
          project-id: ${{ matrix.project.id }}
          corpus-path: ${{ steps.paths.outputs.corpus }}
          pr-number: ${{ needs.discover.outputs.pr-number }}


      - name: Fuzz
        id: fuzz
        continue-on-error: true
        uses: ./.github/actions/moon-run
        with:
          task: ${{ matrix.project.id }}:fuzz-ci

      - name: Triage
        if: steps.fuzz.outcome == 'failure'
        uses: ./.github/actions/moon-run
        with:
          task: ${{ matrix.project.id }}:fuzz-triage

      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: steps.fuzz.outcome == 'failure'
        with:
          name: fuzz-regressions-${{ matrix.project.id }}
          path: ${{ steps.paths.outputs.regressions }}/
          retention-days: 30

      - name: Fail if crashes
        if: steps.fuzz.outcome == 'failure'
        run: exit 1

  report:
    needs: [discover, saturate]
    if: always() && needs.discover.result == 'success'
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 10
    permissions:
      contents: write
      pull-requests: write
      statuses: write

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.discover.outputs.ref }}
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Build target map
        if: needs.saturate.result == 'failure'
        id: targets
        run: |
          TARGETS=$(echo '${{ needs.discover.outputs.results }}' \
            | jq -c '[.[] | {
                key: .id,
                value: (.source + "/" + (.metadata["regressions-path"] // "regressions"))
              }] | from_entries')
          echo "map=${TARGETS}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - id: stage
        if: needs.saturate.result == 'failure'
        uses: ./.github/actions/git-stage-artifacts
        with:
          artifact-prefix: fuzz-regressions
          targets: ${{ steps.targets.outputs.map }}

      - id: commit
        if: steps.stage.outputs.file-count != '0'
        uses: ./.github/actions/git-commit-push
        with:
          add-paths: "**/regressions/"
          message: "chore(fuzz): add ${{ steps.stage.outputs.file-count }} regression files from saturation run"

      - name: Build comment
        if: always()
        id: comment
        run: |
          SATURATE="${{ needs.saturate.result }}"
          COMMITTED="${{ steps.commit.outputs.committed }}"
          FILECOUNT="${{ steps.commit.outputs.file-count }}"
          PROJECTS="${{ steps.stage.outputs.projects }}"
          RUN_URL="${GITHUB_SERVER_URL}/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY}/actions/runs/${GITHUB_RUN_ID}"

          if [[ "$SATURATE" == "success" ]]; then
            STATE="success"
            DESC="No crashes found"
            read -r -d '' BODY <<MD || true
          ## 🟢 Fuzz Saturate: clean

          No crashes found. [View run](${RUN_URL})
          MD

          elif [[ "$COMMITTED" == "true" ]]; then
            STATE="failure"
            DESC="Crashes found. ${FILECOUNT} regressions committed."
            read -r -d '' BODY <<MD || true
          ## 🔴 Fuzz Saturate: crashes found

          **${FILECOUNT} regression files** triaged and committed to this branch.
          Regression inputs are under \`**/regressions/\` and validated by the existing test suite.

          Projects: \`${PROJECTS}\`

          [View run](${RUN_URL}) · [Download crash artifacts](${RUN_URL}#artifacts)
          MD

          elif [[ "$SATURATE" == "skipped" ]]; then
            STATE="success"
            DESC="No affected fuzz projects"
            read -r -d '' BODY <<MD || true
          ## ⚪ Fuzz Saturate: skipped

          No affected projects with \`fuzz-ci\` task. [View run](${RUN_URL})
          MD

          else
            STATE="failure"
            DESC="Fuzz run did not complete normally"
            read -r -d '' BODY <<MD || true
          ## 🟠 Fuzz Saturate: error

          Fuzz run did not complete normally. [View run](${RUN_URL})
          MD
          fi

          {
            echo "state=${STATE}"
            echo "description=${DESC}"
            echo "body<<EOF"
            echo "${BODY}"
            echo "EOF"
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - uses: ./.github/actions/pr-set-status
        if: always()
        with:
          sha: ${{ needs.discover.outputs.sha }}
          pr-number: ${{ needs.discover.outputs.pr-number }}
          state: ${{ steps.comment.outputs.state }}
          context: "fuzz / saturate"
          description: ${{ steps.comment.outputs.description }}
          comment-marker: "<!-- fuzz-saturate -->"
          comment-body: ${{ steps.comment.outputs.body }}
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Fail if crashes
        if: needs.saturate.result == 'failure'
        run: exit 1
########## END: workflows/pr-fuzz.yml ##########

########## FILE: workflows/pr.yml ##########
name: Pull Request Pipeline

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, ready_for_review]

env:
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true

concurrency:
  group: pr-${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  config:
    name: CI Strategy
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      ci-matrix: ${{ steps.ci-matrix.outputs.matrix }}
      runner-count: ${{ steps.ci-matrix.outputs.count }}
    steps:
      # Shallow checkout so GH Actions can resolve local composite actions.
      # The setup action re-checks out with full history for moon's affected detection.
      # TODO: Remove once composite actions are published to a shared repo.
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup
      - id: ci-matrix
        uses: ./.github/actions/moon-ci-matrix

  ci:
    name: Moon CI
    needs: [config]
    if: ${{ fromJson(needs.config.outputs.runner-count || '0') > 0 }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      fail-fast: true
      matrix:
        shard: ${{ fromJson(needs.config.outputs.ci-matrix) }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup
      - uses: ./.github/actions/moon-ci
        with:
          access-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

  audit:
    name: Audit & SCA
    needs: [ci]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup
      - name: Run Moon Audit Tasks (OSV, Cargo, Gitleaks)
        run: moon run :audit --affected

  codeql:
    name: CodeQL
    needs: [ci]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      security-events: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup
        with:
          cache-cargo: "false"
          cache-pnpm: "false"
      - uses: github/codeql-action/init@v3
        with:
          languages: javascript
      - uses: github/codeql-action/autobuild@v3
      - uses: github/codeql-action/analyze@v3

  # will evaluate when closer to a release
  # semgrep:
  #   name: Semgrep
  #   needs: [ci]
  #   runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  #   permissions:
  #     security-events: write
  #   container:
  #     image: returntocorp/semgrep
  #   steps:
  #     - uses: actions/checkout@v4
  #     - name: Run Semgrep scan
  #       run: |
  #         semgrep ci \
  #           --sarif --output=semgrep.sarif \
  #           --config="p/default" \
  #           --config="p/security-audit" \
  #           --config="p/secrets"
  #       env:
  #         SEMGREP_APP_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.SEMGREP_APP_TOKEN }}
  #     - name: Upload SARIF file
  #       uses: github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3
  #       if: always()
  #       with:
  #         sarif_file: semgrep.sarif
  #         category: semgrep

  gate:
    name: PR Gate
    if: always()
    needs: [ci, audit, codeql]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - run: |
          if [[ "${{ needs.ci.result }}" == "failure" || \
                "${{ needs.audit.result }}" == "failure" || \
                "${{ needs.codeql.result }}" == "failure" ]]; then
            exit 1
          fi
        shell: bash

  # Review App & Artifacts (RAA)
  review-open:
    name: Review Build
    if: contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'review-app')
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [ci]
    permissions:
      deployments: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup

      - id: raa
        uses: ./.github/actions/moon-raa
        with:
          command: run
          pr-number: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
          github-token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Summary
        if: always()
        run: echo '${{ steps.raa.outputs.summary }}' >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"

########## END: workflows/pr.yml ##########

########## FILE: workflows/publish.yml ##########
name: Publish Distribution

on:
  release:
    types: [published]
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      scope:
        description: "What to publish/review"
        required: true
        type: choice
        options:
          - affected
          - all
      snapshot:
        description: "Run as a snapshot build (no registry publish)"
        required: true
        type: boolean
        default: true

env:
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true

jobs:
  router:
    name: Determine Target
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      task: ${{ steps.parse.outputs.task }}
      target: ${{ steps.parse.outputs.target }}
      environment: ${{ steps.parse.outputs.environment }}
    steps:
      - id: parse
        run: |
          TASK="publish"
          TARGET=""
          ENV=""

          if [[ "$EVENT_NAME" == "workflow_dispatch" ]]; then
            if [[ "$INPUT_SNAPSHOT" == "true" ]]; then
              TASK="review"
            fi

            if [[ "$INPUT_SCOPE" == "affected" ]]; then
              TARGET=":${TASK} --affected"
            else
              TARGET=":${TASK}"
            fi
          fi

          if [[ "$EVENT_NAME" == "release" ]]; then
            COMP=$(echo "$RELEASE_TAG" | sed -E 's/-v[0-9].*//')

            if [[ "$RELEASE_PRERELEASE" == "true" ]]; then
              TASK="review"
            else
              TASK="publish"
              ENV="release"
            fi

            TARGET="${COMP}:${TASK}"
          fi

          {
            echo "task=${TASK}"
            echo "target=${TARGET}"
            echo "environment=${ENV}"
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
        env:
          EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}
          INPUT_SNAPSHOT: ${{ inputs.snapshot }}
          INPUT_SCOPE: ${{ inputs.scope }}
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}
          RELEASE_PRERELEASE: ${{ github.event.release.prerelease }}
  publish:
    name: Moon Run ${{ needs.router.outputs.target }}
    needs: router
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: ${{ needs.router.outputs.environment }}
    permissions:
      contents: write
      packages: write
    steps:
      # Shallow checkout so GH Actions can resolve local composite actions.
      # The setup action re-checks out with full history for moon's affected detection.
      # TODO: Remove once composite actions are published to a shared repo.
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1
      - uses: ./.github/actions/setup

      - name: Execute Moon Task
        run: moon run ${{ needs.router.outputs.target }}
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN }}
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
          PYPI_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.PYPI_API_TOKEN }}

      - name: Attach standalone artifacts to release
        if: github.event_name == 'release'
        run: |
          if [ -d dist/ ]; then
            gh release upload "${{ github.event.release.tag_name }}" dist/* --clobber
          fi
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

########## END: workflows/publish.yml ##########
